summary: All four FRs verified by reading code and running task verification commands. json.load loader correct, FileNotFoundError handled with stderr message and exit code 1, phases.json contains 7 phases with full structure, PHASES literal absent from game.py. One minor edge-case finding (unhandled JSONDecodeError) noted but explicitly excluded by spec Non-Goals.

findings.description: The try/except block at game.py:354-359 only catches FileNotFoundError. A corrupt phases.json raises json.JSONDecodeError which escapes as an unhandled traceback. This is outside the spec scope (Non-Goals explicitly exclude JSON-Schema-Validierung) but is a user-experience gap.
findings.evidence: except FileNotFoundError: — no except for json.JSONDecodeError
findings.suggestion: Add: except json.JSONDecodeError as e: print(f'Fehler: phases.json ist kein valides JSON: {e}', file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1) — in a follow-on feature if desired.

findings.description: Error message uses a space instead of newline between sentences vs. the tasks.md template. AC requires only 'verständliche Fehlermeldung' — passes.
findings.evidence: 'Fehler: phases.json nicht gefunden. Erwartet: ...' (single space, not newline as shown in tasks.md:120)

requirements_checked.evidence: game.py:357-359 — FileNotFoundError caught, message to stderr includes path, sys.exit(1)
requirements_checked.notes: Message is on one line vs. two-line template in tasks.md, but AC only requires readable message

requirements_checked.evidence: phases.json verified: 7 phases, all keys (name, zweck, kernfrage, prompt, interaktion, beispiel, fragen) and sub-keys present

requirements_checked.notes: T-002 verification command also asserts this

verified_clean.result: json imported correctly at module level after unicodedata

verified_clean.method: File list review, grep for unexpected changes
verified_clean.result: Only game.py and phases.json changed, no drive-by cleanup

verified_clean.method: Programmatic key check against spec-defined schema
verified_clean.result: All 7 phases have all required top-level and nested keys
